codeReview.summary: Streaming implementation correctly delivers token-by-token responses via SSE. The POST /api/sessions/{id}/messages/stream endpoint yields properly typed events ({'type': 'token', 'content': ...}, {'type': 'session_state', ...}, {'type': 'complete', ...}, {'type': 'error', ...}). StreamHandler callbacks work correctly. UI updates incrementally via on_token callback. Tests pass. Note: a subsequent fix commit (1d812bc) corrected error event formatting that had been malformed in the original commit.

issues.description: Tests only cover StreamHandler class initialization - they verify callbacks can be assigned but don't test actual SSE parsing, token accumulation, or error handling paths. Integration tests for the streaming endpoint itself are missing. However, user-testing validation passed, confirming end-to-end streaming works.

The on_error callback is defined but does nothing (pass). While errors are returned via the main call's error return value and handled appropriately, a no-op error callback could mislead future developers. Consider logging errors or showing them in the UI.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill and AGENTS.md don't specify a standard SSE event format convention. The worker implemented events with a `type` field (token, session_state, complete, error) which is consistent and worked well. However, the original error event format was missing the `type` field, causing silent error swallowing - this was caught and fixed in a subsequent commit (1d812bc). A documented SSE convention in AGENTS.md would prevent this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: session.py:302, :318 originally used {'error': '...'} without type field - fixed to {'type': 'error', 'error': '...'}. Other events (token, session_state, complete) correctly use type field.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill procedure mentions writing tests but doesn't provide guidance on testing SSE/streaming endpoints specifically. The worker followed the procedure but the resulting tests only cover class instantiation, not actual streaming behavior. However, user-testing validation passed, confirming the streaming works end-to-end.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test_streaming.py contains only 3 tests, all testing StreamHandler initialization - no SSE parsing or integration tests. But user-testing validator confirmed tokens stream correctly.

summary: Streaming feature correctly implements token-by-token LLM responses with SSE. Backend streams properly typed events (token, session_state, complete, error). StreamHandler and API client handle events correctly. UI displays tokens incrementally via on_token callback. All tests pass. The prior review identified error event format bug - this was fixed in commit 1d812bc. Minor issue: test coverage only validates StreamHandler initialization, not actual streaming behavior, though user-testing validation confirms end-to-end functionality.
